description: Three design choices are well-executed and worth defending: (1) Opening a fresh ESAPI Context per call instead of caching — Context is !Send, so this avoids unsafe gymnastics. The struct doc correctly explains the rationale. (2) Software pre-hash in sign() eliminates the MaxBuffer size limit transparently to callers, which matches the trait contract (sign takes arbitrary &[u8]). (3) The three inventory factories each gate on mode, preventing confusing runtime errors when a user accidentally calls get() on a signing key URI through the dispatch system. The separation of concerns (NV vs signing) is consistently enforced at both construction and call sites.
title: Clean architecture: context-per-call, software pre-hash, inventory gating
